ocr.text: In the evaluation of patient health, comprehensive laboratory analyses play a crucial role in guiding clinical decision-making
and treatment strategies. The data presented in the table illustrates key findings from multiple laboratory tests, including arterial
blood gas analysis, complete blood cell count, and biochemistry assessments. Notably, the arterial blood gas analysis indicates a
pH level of 7.431, which falls within the normal physiological range, suggesting adequate acid-base balance in the patient. This
metric is essential for assessing respiratory and metabolic function, particularly in patients requiring supplemental oxygen
therapy, as indicated by the administration of 3L/min of oxygen.
Arterial blood gas analysis (3L/min of oxygen)  Complete blood cell count  Biochemistry  Coagulation
pH  7.431  WBC  8310/μL  TP  5.6 g/dL  PT  56%
PaCO2  30.6 mmHg  NE  97.9%  Albumin  3.1 g/dL  INR  1.31
PaO2  93.5 mmHg  LY  1.1%  AST  20 U/L  APTT  28 s
HCO3−  20 mmol/L  MONO  0.4%  ALT  9 U/L  Fibrinogen  181 mg/dL
Base excess  −3.2 mmol/L  Hgb  9.2 g/dL  T-Bil  2.08 mg/dL  D-dimer  6.1 μg/mL
Lactate  5 mmol/L  Hct  27.1%  D-Bil  0.83 mg/dL  AT3  58%
Glucose  200 mg/dL  PLT  9.6 × 104/μL  BUN  17.3 mg/dL
Creatinine  1.20 mg/dL
Na  136 mmol/L
K  3.4 mmol/L
Cl  106 mmol/L
CRP  0.86 mg/dL
Procalcitonin  39.27 ng/mL
Endotoxin  ≤0.8 pg/mL
Additionally, the complete blood cell count reveals a white blood cell (WBC) count of 8310/μL, which may suggest the
presence of an underlying infection or inflammatory process that warrants further investigation. Furthermore, the biochemistry
results, including total protein (TP) levels, provide insight into the patient’s nutritional status and liver function. Collectively,
these laboratory findings offer a comprehensive overview of the patient's health status and serve as a vital resource for the
clinical team in formulating an effective management plan.
